product.name: Cognit
product.definition: Persistent Decision and Knowledge Layer for AI Engineering

principles.principle: Store events, evidence, decisions, and verification results.
Workers are temporary compute. Cognit owns state.
Identity is per-actor, not per-event. Trust starts at the actor boundary.
Schema versions are explicit. Old events must remain replayable forever.
Privacy is a default, not a feature — redact on ingest, not on read.
No speculative fields. Add columns when a use case appears, not before.
Local-only tool — no auth, no login, no token, no cookie. The loopback bind is the security boundary. Docker compose binds 0.0.0.0 but never publishes the port to the host.

scope.goal: Build a local-first Cognit runtime for recording, recovering, and visualizing AI engineering cognition.
scope.note: Terminology: this spec uses three release tags. "Bootstrap" = <bootstrap_phases>, phases 0-4: event store, CLI, reducer, verification, export/import. No API, no dashboard. "v0.1" = Bootstrap + <v0_1_phases> (phases 5-6): Hono API + Vite dashboard. "v0.2" = v0.1 + <v0_2_phases> (phases 7-9): recovery engine, gravity, constraint engine, inbox adapter, cognit wrap. The README uses "MVP" to mean v0.1; treat the two as synonyms.

standards.note: All technical decisions (runtime, package manager, monorepo tool, frameworks, FP runtime, ORM, test runner, build tool, linter, formatter) live in STACK.md. Naming, file layout, code style, and anti-patterns live in CONVENTIONS.md. Add new tech decisions to those files, not here. This scope block intentionally stays minimal so the data model and feature spec remain the focus of plan.xml.

sections.section: name (defaults to directory name at init time)
enabled (default true), extra patterns (name, regex, replacement) layered on top of built-ins
defaults per type (human|worker|system), per-actor overrides (name, trust_score)

config.note: No `auth:` or `server:` section. Cognit is local-only — there is no token, no cookie, no auth gate. The server binds loopback by default; docker compose overrides the bind host but never publishes the port.

entity.name: project
entity.description: The top-level scope. In local-first mode, one project per .cognit directory, typically one git repository. Project name is captured at init time from the directory name (or --project flag) and stored in the projects table and cognit.yaml.

entity.name: session
entity.description: One investigation or engineering goal. A resumed session becomes a new session that links back via parent_session_id (unless --fork=false).
entity.relationship: belongs to one project; may have one parent_session_id (when resumed/forked)

entity.name: actor
entity.description: A source of events: human, worker AI, or system. Identity is first-class so the future trust engine can reason about it. Unknown actors seen for the first time are auto-registered with a default trust_score from cognit.yaml.
entity.relationship: authored by events; events carry actor_id (FK)

entity.name: observation
entity.description: Raw fact from terminal, logs, code, benchmark, human, or AI.

entity.name: finding
entity.description: Interpreted knowledge from one or more observations.
entity.example: Memory growth starts after HMR updates.

entity.name: hypothesis
entity.description: Testable explanation. Has a lifecycle: active → weakened | rejected | promoted.

states.state: rejected (with reason_type: evidence | superseded | constraint; and optional superseded_by_id)

entity.name: theory
entity.description: Group of related hypotheses, organized around a theme. First-class entity — not a label. Mergeable, archivable.

entity.name: experiment
entity.description: Action designed to validate, weaken, or reject a hypothesis. Always linked to the hypothesis it tests via a tests edge.
entity.example: Disable Turbopack and measure memory growth.

entity.name: decision
entity.description: An action commitment. Distinct from conclusion: a decision is "we will do X", a conclusion is "X is true". A decision is based_on one or more conclusions.

entity.name: conclusion
entity.description: A verified claim about the world, backed by evidence and verification. Only verified conclusions can ground a decision.
entity.example: Turbopack is not the cause of the memory leak.

entity.name: verification
entity.description: Lint, typecheck, test, build, benchmark, or reproducible validation. Complete lifecycle.

states.state: errored (command could not run: file missing, permission denied, sandbox violation)

entity.name: artifact
entity.description: Attached evidence file, addressed by content hash (sha256).

entity.name: edge
entity.description: A typed relationship between two entities. Stored explicitly so the knowledge graph and decision lineage are queryable, not reconstructed by scanning events.

edge_types.type: experiment → hypothesis. Verifications link to a hypothesis via the linked_hypothesis_id field on the verification record, NOT via an edge_created event — a verification is the result of a test that was run separately, not a test itself.

event_shape.field: ulid FK (replaces the old actor_json blob)
schema version of the event payload (semver). Used to migrate old events on replay.
type-specific shape, validated by Effect Schema at ingest
tool / command / file path that produced the event (kept as JSON for flexibility)
array of artifact ids this event references
event id that directly caused this event (nullable)
real 0-1, set at event creation; entity-level confidence is derived from the latest confidence-bearing event for that entity
ulid FK nullable, set on verification_rerun

event_types.event: experiment_completed (payload: result_summary, supports: array of hypothesis_ids, contradicts: array of hypothesis_ids)
edge_created (payload: edge_type, from_entity_type, from_entity_id, to_entity_type, to_entity_id)
actor_registered (payload: actor_type, actor_name, trust_score; emitted when a new actor id is seen for the first time — by the CLI, inbox watcher, or SDK)
redaction_applied (audit trail for what was redacted, never the redacted content)

event_naming_convention.rule: Each state transition of an entity is a distinct event type (e.g. hypothesis_rejected, decision_accepted), not a generic "status_changed" with a payload field.
event_naming_convention.rationale: - Lets event consumers (dashboard, recovery, constraint engine, subscription filters) handle specific transitions without parsing payloads. - Makes the event log self-describing in tools like grep and SQL: SELECT * FROM events WHERE type = 'hypothesis_rejected' is unambiguous. - Avoids ambiguity when reading historical events years later — the type itself carries the semantic.
event_naming_convention.exception: The polymorphic "edge_created" carries edge_type in its payload. Edges are inherently typed (tests, supports, based_on, ...) and a single event type with a typed payload is cleaner than N event types for N edge types, and matches the storage shape of the edges table. "Hypothesis in theory T1" is a relationship, not a hypothesis state transition — the CLI flag --belongs-to emits a single edge_created(belongs_to) and the edges table is the source of truth. There is no hypothesis_belongs_to_theory event type.
event_naming_convention.consistency_check: - hypothesis: created, weakened, rejected, promoted - theory: created, updated, merged, archived - decision: proposed, accepted, rejected, superseded - conclusion: proposed, verified, rejected - session: created, paused, closed - verification: started, passed, failed, errored, cancelled, rerun All state transitions within an entity are distinct event types. The list above is the contract.

table.name: sessions
table.column: project_id text not null references projects(id)
status text check status in (active, paused, closed)

table.name: actors
table.column: type text check type in (human, worker, system)

table.name: events
table.column: project_id text not null references projects(id)
session_id text not null references sessions(id)
actor_id text not null references actors(id)
linked_hypothesis_id text nullable references hypotheses(id) (set when a verification is created via cognit verify --tests, so the dashboard can group rerun history per hypothesis)

table.name: snapshots
table.column: session_id text not null references sessions(id)
event_id text not null references events(id)

table.name: artifacts
table.column: session_id text not null references sessions(id)

table.name: edges
table.column: session_id text not null references sessions(id)

state_reducer.description: Rebuild session state from events. Confidence on entities is derived from the latest confidence-bearing event. When last_snapshot_event_id is set, restore the snapshot and replay only events after it.

state_sections.section: verifications (with state machine and rerun history)
graph_nodes and graph_edges (derived view for dashboard rendering)

cli.convention: All entity-referencing commands accept --id <ulid> OR a title substring. Use --id for precision. Title match is fuzzy (fuse.js) and errors out when ambiguous. The current session is sticky after cognit session create; pass --session to override.

commands.command: cognit session resume "goal-or-id" [--fork=true] (default fork=true: creates new session with parent_session_id; if multiple open sessions match the goal, the most recently created one is picked and a warning is printed; use --id <ulid> for precision)
cognit session show <id-or-goal> (reducer output: rejected hypotheses, verified conclusions, accepted decisions, last known state, all events in order)
cognit edge add --type supports|contradicts|tests|... --from entity:id --to entity:id
cognit edge list [--session id] [--type edge-type] [--from entity:id] [--to entity:id] (read-only dump of the edges table)

cognit events [--session id] [--type event-type] [--actor name] [--since iso] [--limit n] [--follow] (terminal tail of the event stream; --follow polls SQLite directly via packages/db — no API server required; works from Bootstrap onward)
cognit redaction test "raw string" (dry-run every configured + built-in pattern against the input; prints which patterns matched and what the redacted output would be, without writing to the store)
cognit wrap -- claude-code ... (wraps any worker command, captures observations automatically)

api.note: The SSE endpoint GET /events/stream powers the dashboard Timeline page live mode. The CLI cognit events --follow reads SQLite directly and does NOT use this endpoint — they are independent implementations of the same concept. This separation means --follow works without the API server running.

route.note: SSE; pushes new events as they are appended. Powers the Timeline page live mode in the dashboard only. The CLI --follow flag reads SQLite directly, not this endpoint.

page.name: Overview
page.shows: goal, confidence, progress, current strongest hypothesis, latest verification

page.name: Timeline
page.shows: event stream evolution, filterable by type and actor

page.name: Knowledge Graph
page.shows: all entities as nodes, all edge types as links; layout toggle between free and physics ("constellation")
page.note: Replaces the old separate "Constellation" page. Constellation was the same graph with a different layout.

page.name: Decision Graph
page.shows: proposed/accepted/rejected/superseded decisions, with based_on edges to conclusions, and caused edges to experiments

page.name: Verification
page.shows: all verifications with their final state (passed/failed/errored/cancelled), with rerun history per hypothesis

page.name: Recovery Center
page.shows: related sessions, rejected hypotheses (with reason_type), verified conclusions, accepted decisions, suggested next steps

page.name: Settings
page.shows: project config, redaction patterns, cleanup policy, storage usage, export/import buttons

verification_engine.description: Run local commands and store reproducible verification results with a complete lifecycle.

lifecycle.state: errored (could not run: file not found, permission denied, sandbox violation)
cancelled (user or worker killed it; recorded for forensic completeness)

verification_engine.rerun: A new verification_rerun event is appended, with parent_verification_id pointing to the previous run. The dashboard shows rerun history per hypothesis.

captures.item: stdout (truncated to 1 MB; full version stored as artifact)
stderr (truncated to 1 MB; full version stored as artifact)

recovery_engine.description: Resume useful cognition without loading old chat. Ships in v0.2 (Phase 7). Bootstrap (Phase 2) ships a minimal recovery block: rejected hypotheses, verified conclusions, accepted decisions, no fuzzy search, no suggested next steps. Semantic search is post-v0.2.
recovery_engine.search: Fuzzy keyword search (fuse.js) over session goals, findings, hypotheses, decisions, conclusions. Filter by status, project, and minimum confidence.

export_import.use_cases: Backup, transfer between machines, share a session with a teammate.
export_import.format: SQLite dump + artifacts/ directory + cognit.yaml, tar.gz compressed.

gravity_engine.status: Ships in v0.2 (Phase 8). Simple weighted sum. Reads from derived entity state (not raw events).

inputs.input: reproducibility (count of passed verifications, weighted by recency)
actor_trust (weighted by actor.trust_score of contributing events)
freshness (decay: older unverified claims lose score)

gravity_engine.note: Freshness decay prevents stale, never-verified hypotheses from dominating the queue.

constraint_engine.description: Rule-based pruning. Renamed from "entanglement" — the internal model is a plain rule engine and "entanglement" implied something it isn't.
constraint_engine.status: Ships in v0.2 (Phase 8). Rule-based pruning, fired on experiment_completed and verification_failed events.

rule_shape.field: condition_json — JSON object using the condition DSL defined below
actions_json — JSON array of action objects defined below

full_example.description: Reject a low-confidence active hypothesis once a completed experiment directly contradicts it.

secret_redaction.description: Apply redaction patterns at event ingest. Configured in cognit.yaml. Built-in patterns are always on; users can add more. Every redaction produces a redaction_applied event with the pattern name and the entity affected, but never the redacted content. Redaction runs inside the `appendEvent` function — every write path (inbox watcher, CLI direct write, SDK call, future HTTP POST /events) goes through it. The inbox watcher does NOT redact on its own; it just feeds untrusted bytes into appendEvent. If a future contributor adds a new write path, the only safe assumption is "appendEvent is the redaction boundary". Redaction hits are recorded with a non-empty `field_path` of the form `payload.value.` or `source.value.`. The `value` segment is the wrapper key used by `redactEvent` to thread the path through `scanValue`; the redacted `payload_json` shape is unchanged.
secret_redaction.phase: Phase 1 (event store), not future — secrets should never reach the store in the first place.

data_lifecycle.description: Prevent unbounded growth of the local store.

artifact_policy.referenced_by: an edge of type references pointing from any active conclusion or accepted decision

db_growth.max_db_size_mb: 1024 (configurable; warning at 80%, hard stop at 100% with "run cognit gc" prompt)

event_compaction.status: Post-v0.2. For v0.1, full event stream is retained. Compaction would create a snapshot and prune old events behind it, with a one-line caveat in the recovery output.

worker_adapter.description: Any AI CLI can publish events by writing JSON into .cognit/inbox. Includes atomic write protocol, redaction step, and auto-actor-registration. Ships in v0.2 (Phase 9). The v0.1 onboarding is direct file writes into the inbox by the worker, or CLI commands issued by the human — neither path is supported by the watcher.

concurrency.rule: Single writer per session. Multiple writers across sessions is fine. The watcher uses chokidar with atomic-write detection: a file is considered complete when its name no longer has a .tmp suffix AND its mtime is older than debounce_ms (default 200).

atomic_write_protocol.step: 3. Rename to .cognit/inbox/<session-id>-<ulid>.json (atomic on POSIX, near-atomic on Windows)

failure_handling.actor_not_registered: Auto-register with default trust_score from cognit.yaml actors.defaults.<type>; emit actor_registered event. trust_score 0 is a sentinel for "unset" — never allow it to persist.

redaction_step.where: Applied by `appendEvent` (in packages/db), regardless of source. The watcher only validates JSON shape, Effect Schema, and atomic-write detection — redaction is the responsibility of the store layer, not the watcher. This guarantees the same invariant for CLI direct writes, SDK calls, and future HTTP endpoints.

auto_capture.usage: cognit wrap -- claude-code ... captures tool calls, exit codes, and stderr lines as observations
auto_capture.claude_code_hooks: Document .claude/settings.json PostToolUse → cognit observation add; PreToolUse → cognit hypothesis add if file is unfamiliar

inbox_to_store_mapping.field: serialized to source_json (text) on appendEvent; shape is {tool, command, file_path?}
advisory only; appendEvent overwrites with server-side timestamp. Including it in the inbox file is allowed but ignored.
passed through verbatim to event columns
stored on the event row's confidence column. NOT a payload field.
resolved to actor_id via actors table; auto-registers unknown names with type default trust_score from cognit.yaml.actors.defaults. trust_score 0 is a sentinel — always overwrite on registration.

validated by Effect Schema per event type and version, then redacted by appendEvent. Payload contains type-specific fields only (e.g. {title} for hypothesis_created); cross-cutting fields (confidence, source, causation_id, correlation_id, created_at) are top-level on the event, not payload fields.

bootstrap_phases.description: True minimum that validates the concept. If you only have a weekend, ship this. No API, no dashboard, no recovery search — just a working event store, CLI, and one human-readable end-of-session report. Phases 0-4 only.

phase.name: Bootstrap
phase.done_when: cognit init creates local Cognit project, writes cognit.yaml and .gitignore

phase.name: Database and Event Store
phase.task: Create SQLite schema (projects, sessions, actors, events, snapshots, artifacts, edges)
Implement redaction pipeline (apply built-in and config patterns at ingest)
Implement appendEvent with Effect Schema validation per event type and per version
Implement event schema migration utility (replay-time version transform)
Add packages/core/__fixtures__/events-v0.x.json (a small hand-written event log from a fictional previous version) and a reducer replay test that asserts the migrated state matches the snapshot
phase.done_when: Events can be appended with redaction and replayed across schema versions; a v0.x fixture replays cleanly into the current schema

phase.name: Session Runtime and Reducer
phase.task: Create session commands (create, list, resume, pause, close, show)
Implement resume-as-fork (sets parent_session_id, inherits context summary)
Implement reducer with snapshot policy (every 100 events + on close)
Implement session state rebuild from events + last snapshot
phase.done_when: cognit session resume can rebuild state from snapshot + tail events

phase.name: Cognition Commands
phase.task: Implement edge add and edge list (typed relationships)
Implement hypothesis state transitions (weaken/reject with reason_type)
phase.done_when: Core investigation flow works from CLI with full entity relationships and edge graph

phase.name: Verification + Data Lifecycle
phase.task: Implement cognit gc (artifact archive, db size warning)
Implement cognit export/import (tar.gz bundle, lossless round-trip)
phase.done_when: A session can run a full investigation, produce verifiable evidence, export itself, and be re-imported on another machine

v0_1_phases.description: Adds API + dashboard on top of Bootstrap. The README calls this "MVP" — the two are synonyms. Phases 5-6. The dashboard's Recovery Center and Overview pages will render with reduced content in v0.1 (the full recovery block and gravity ranking ship in v0.2); v0.1 should gate those widgets behind a "v0.2" badge or omit them.

phase.name: API Server
phase.task: Expose state, graph, recovery, edges endpoints
Implement GET /events/stream SSE endpoint for dashboard Timeline live mode
phase.done_when: Dashboard can read Cognit state through HTTP

phase.name: Dashboard
phase.task: Create Knowledge Graph page (with physics/constellation layout toggle)
Create Decision Graph page (with based_on edges)
Create Settings page (config editor, redaction patterns, storage usage, export/import)
phase.done_when: cognit dashboard opens http://localhost:6970 and shows a complete session

v0_2_phases.description: Advanced features. Each phase is independently shippable. Phases 7-9. Phase 7 unlocks the full recovery output (fuzzy search + the "Last known state" and "Suggested next step" lines). Phase 8 adds gravity ranking and the constraint engine. Phase 9 lands the inbox adapter and cognit wrap, completing the worker-agnostic story.

phase.name: Recovery Engine
phase.task: Fuzzy keyword search (fuse.js) over goals, findings, hypotheses, decisions, conclusions
Build recovery output (related_sessions, rejected_hypotheses, accepted_decisions, suggested_next_steps)
phase.done_when: Old investigation can be resumed without chat context

phase.name: Gravity and Constraint
phase.task: Apply freshness decay to never-verified hypotheses
Implement constraint rule JSON DSL (condition_dsl + actions_dsl as specified in constraint_engine)
Apply rules on experiment_completed and verification_failed events
phase.done_when: One experiment can weaken/reject/promote related hypotheses via a rule

phase.name: Worker Inbox Adapter and Auto-capture
phase.task: Auto-register unknown actors with default trust_score from cognit.yaml; emit actor_registered event
Implement cognit wrap for arbitrary worker commands
Document Claude Code / Codex / OpenCode / Gemini CLI hooks
phase.done_when: Any external AI CLI can publish Cognit events, and cognit wrap can capture tool calls automatically

future_direction.item: Semantic search using local embeddings for better recovery (post-v0.2).
Git integration: link sessions to branches, commits, and diffs; surface "what was I investigating when this commit landed".
Plugin system for Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini CLI, and custom workers (built on top of the inbox adapter).
Trust engine: per-actor trust with history; ability for a worker to disagree with the trust assigned to it.
Remote sync for team usage (CRDTs or append-only log replication).
Collaboration with shared project cognition and per-actor write permissions.
Supervisor mode: a long-running worker that observes, ranks, assigns, verifies, prunes, and resumes investigations.
Event compaction and archival beyond the simple gc.
Artifact encryption at rest (now that redaction is in place to keep plaintext out of events).
Multi-tenant workspaces (add workspace_id back as a schema migration).
Web dashboard for shared/team mode (v0.1 dashboard is single-user local).

bootstrap_success_criteria.criterion: cognit init creates a project and writes cognit.yaml and .gitignore
A session can run a full investigation (observation → finding → hypothesis → experiment → verification → decision) from the CLI

Experiments are linked to the hypotheses they test; verification results impact those hypotheses (verifications link to hypotheses via the linked_hypothesis_id field on the verification record, not via a tests edge)
cognit session resume forks a new session with parent_session_id and emits a basic recovery block (rejected hypotheses, verified conclusions, accepted decisions)
Secrets (JWT, API keys, PEM blocks, password fields) are redacted at ingest and never appear in events
cognit export/import round-trips a session losslessly
cognit events --follow tails the event stream directly from SQLite without requiring the API server

v0_1_success_criteria.criterion: Dashboard opens on port 6970 and shows Overview, Timeline, Knowledge Graph, Decision Graph, Verification, Settings (Recovery Center renders with a "v0.2" badge or is omitted — the full recovery output block and gravity ranking ship in v0.2)
API serves state, graph, edges, and a minimal recovery endpoint (just rejected_hypotheses, verified_conclusions, accepted_decisions — fuzzy search and suggested_next_steps are v0.2)
Snapshots are created automatically and used to speed up state rebuild
GET /events/stream SSE endpoint pushes live events to the dashboard Timeline page
